---
title: Key concepts
---

A handful of words appear throughout this manual. This page defines each one in
plain language, in the order you meet it, so the steps make sense when you
reach them.

## model.json

A file that describes the structure of a BigPicture-compatible dataset: what
types of data it can contain (specimens, observations, images) and how they
relate to each other. For most sites the provided default works unchanged.

→ You supply this file at [**E. Reference data**](reference-data.qmd).

## Mapper workbook (`mappers.xlsx`)

A spreadsheet you fill in for your site that translates your institution's
labels, codes, and field names into the vocabulary BigPicture expects. Each
site has its own mapper workbook.

→ You prepare and provide this at [**E. Reference data**](reference-data.qmd).

## Metadata workbook

The Excel file you create for each submission. It contains the contextual
metadata for your dataset: specimens, observations, whole-slide images, and
their relationships. This is your data — the pipeline ingests it and uses it
to build the submission.

→ You upload this in [**H. Run a submission**](run-submission.qmd).

## Project

A container you create in the SlideTap UI that holds everything for one upload
attempt — its dataset definition and one or more batches. Think of a project as
"one dataset going through the pipeline."

→ You create a project in [**H. Run a submission**](run-submission.qmd).

## Dataset

The named entity within your project that becomes the dataset record in
BigPicture. This is where you define secondary information: the policy, the
metadata standard version, the type of dataset, and so on. The dataset's
identifier (short name) becomes the folder name of your SlideTap outbox.

→ You configure the dataset in [**H. Run a submission**](run-submission.qmd).

## Batch

A processing run within a project where you ingest your metadata workbook and
curate the records through SlideTap. A project can contain multiple batches —
for example if your metadata arrives in parts — though a single batch is
usually enough.

→ You create and complete a batch in [**H. Run a submission**](run-submission.qmd).

## SlideTap outbox

The folder SlideTap writes when a batch finishes its export, named
`DATASET_<your dataset identifier>`. This folder is the direct output of the
pipeline: you validate it, fix any errors, and then upload it to BigPicture.

→ You validate the outbox at [**I. Validate**](validate.qmd) and upload it at
[**J. Upload to BigPicture**](upload.qmd).
